title: Microconomorpha dentata
taxonomy: Microconomorpha dentata is a species of fossilized brachiopod, which is a type of ancient marine animal that belonged to the phylum Brachiopoda. It is classified within the order Terebratulida, which is a group of brachiopods characterized by their thick, calcareous shells. The family to which Microconomorpha dentata belongs is not well-documented, but it is likely to be part of the superfamily Kingenoidea. Further classification of Microconomorpha dentata would require more specific information about its morphology and fossil record. However, it is generally considered to be a member of the kingdom Animalia.
